title: phyteuma globulariifolium subsp. pedemontanum
intro: Phyteuma globulariifolium subsp. pedemontanum is a plant species within the bellflower family (Campanulaceae), specifically a subspecies of the globular-leaved rampion (Phyteuma globulariifolium). It is a perennial herb native to the Italian Alps, where it thrives in alpine and subalpine environments. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, which help alleviate respiratory issues, including coughs and colds, by loosening and expelling mucus. It can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, offering health benefits such as relieving bronchitis and asthma symptoms, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
